description: Machine session state. Provider-neutral: every agent (Claude, Codex, Gemini, or any other) and every human reads this before governed work. Update it at end of session and point active_handoff at the newest handoff.
next_allowed_move: P-FIX-0 through P-FIX-3 are done. Evidence now persists through SqlLedger (evidence_links table, written once at creation, read back correctly) for both events and tasks; TaskInput/router gained an evidence field (previously silently dropped over HTTP). Approval now checks every quorum seat against known-principals.yaml via CvfProfile.known_role_for - a fabricated approver_id or an inflated role for a real-but-lesser id is rejected; this is an interim registry check, NOT real authentication (no signature/token/session), pending P2-B. Both fixes verified with an HTTP probe reproducing the exact Codex scenarios: an R2 event with evidence now confirms instead of being refused, and two fabricated approvers now get 409 instead of confirming an R3 event with 200. Next: P-FIX-4 - add the missing `version` column to migration 002_tasks_customers_reports.sql (tasks table), then tighten test_schema_parity.py to compare exact columns/types/nullability/defaults/PKs/FKs/CHECK expressions instead of just table/FK-table/any-CheckConstraint presence. Follow EXECUTION_ROADMAP.md P-FIX tranche in order. Do NOT open P2-A remaining domains, P2-B, P2-C, or any new phase until the full P-FIX tranche (through P-FIX-5) is done.
commit_discipline: Commit and push to origin main as soon as each roadmap tranche (e.g. P-FIX-1) is complete and gates pass. Do not batch multiple tranches into one commit. Commit message must name the tranche id and verification result.
blocked_work: Reusing the labels 'enforced', '12/12', or 'golden vertical' without the callable/load-bearing/not-verified-server-side distinction from docs/cvf/CVF_CONTROL_MAPPING.md (2026-07-22 correction).
Opening any new roadmap domain or phase (P2-A remaining domains, P2-B, P2-C, Phase 3/4/5 work) before the P-FIX tranche (P-FIX-1 through P-FIX-5) is complete.

Claiming SqlLedger is production-verified on PostgreSQL without an actual PostgreSQL round-trip test run against a migration-created schema (a static defect - missing tasks.version column - was found by the 2026-07-22 review; PostgreSQL is NOT confirmed to be 'the same code path' until P-FIX-4 fixes this and it is verified live).
Claiming cost/termination are load-bearing before an AI mode beyond NO_AI is wired.

Claiming approval identity is fully authenticated - known-principals.yaml only rejects fabricated/inflated approver claims; it is not a signature/token/session check. Do not treat it as equivalent to P2-B real authentication.
Claiming freeze is enforced for report_approved/open_handover_items_linked - those two prerequisites have no model yet and are only satisfied via an explicit, audited override, not a real check.

Trusting InMemoryLedger.get_event/get_task/get_shift to return a live reference in any NEW code - they now return model_copy(); do not reintroduce direct dict access that bypasses transaction() semantics.
Adding provider-named entrypoint files (CLAUDE.md as content, etc.) - front door must stay provider-neutral (CONTRIBUTING.md).
Batching more than one roadmap tranche into a single commit.

verification_snapshot.pytest: 95 passed (verify by rerunning `python -m pytest -q`, do not trust this number without rerunning)
verification_snapshot.make_validate: PASS (catalog + session-state + file-size checks) - catalog check is structural only, does not catch metric/Markdown drift (see P-FIX-5)
verification_snapshot.cvf_controls_callable: 12/12 (see docs/cvf/CVF_CONTROL_MAPPING.md for which are load-bearing vs. callable-only vs. not-verified-server-side)
verification_snapshot.p_fix_1_done: freeze is now load-bearing end-to-end (both backends); see docs/cvf/CVF_CONTROL_MAPPING.md freeze row
verification_snapshot.p_fix_2_done: mutation+audit is now atomic in both backends for all 4 services; see docs/cvf/CVF_CONTROL_MAPPING.md audit row
verification_snapshot.p_fix_3_done: evidence now persists through SqlLedger for events and tasks; approval now rejects fabricated/inflated approver claims via known-principals.yaml (interim, not real auth). See docs/cvf/CVF_CONTROL_MAPPING.md evidence/approval rows
verification_snapshot.known_remaining_defects: PostgreSQL Task insert will fail, migration missing version column (P-FIX-4); catalog --check does not verify metric/Markdown freshness (P-FIX-5)
verification_snapshot.modules_by_status: enforced=2, partial=4, contract-only=6, stub=8 (per generate_catalog.py --check structural validation; not independently re-verified for metric freshness)
verification_snapshot.second_independent_review: docs/decisions/EA_INDEPENDENT_REVIEW_2026-07-22_CODEX.md - read before trusting any 'enforced' claim in this repo
